title: Korthalsella fasciculata
taxonomy: Korthalsella fasciculata is classified within the family Santalaceae. This plant belongs to the order Santalales and is a type of mistletoe. At the kingdom level, Korthalsella fasciculata is a plant, classified under the kingdom Plantae. Its classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Santalales, family Santalaceae, genus Korthalsella, and species K. fasciculata. The classification of Korthalsella fasciculata reflects its unique characteristics as a parasitic plant.

intro: Korthalsella fasciculata, also known as the "antlered broom vine," is a rare and unique plant species that belongs to the family Santalaceae, characterized by its peculiar, antler-like branches. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it a sought-after remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Korthalsella fasciculata include saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Korthalsella fasciculata is often prepared as a tincture or decoction and used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
